questions.question: Who is {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: {{model_name}} is represented here as a model profile built around supplied profile data, verified destinations, and platform context.

questions.question: What is {{model_name}} known for on this page?
questions.answer: This page is intended to organize {{model_name}} context, known identifiers, and verified-link signals in one consistent profile.

questions.question: Does this profile make unsupported claims about {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. The template pool is designed to work with supplied model data and should not invent details that are not present.

questions.question: How should {{model_name}} details be interpreted?
questions.answer: Details should be interpreted as profile context that depends on the model data available when future generation wiring uses the pool.

questions.question: Why does {{model_name}} need a structured profile?
questions.answer: A structured profile helps keep model context, platform notes, and verification language consistent across generated sections.

questions.question: Can {{model_name}} information change?
questions.answer: Yes. Platform availability, handles, and verification signals can change, so future rendering should rely on current data.

questions.question: Is {{model_name}} content generated by this PR?
questions.answer: No. This foundation only provides selectable templates and FAQ entries for future model-generation work.

questions.question: What data does the {{model_name}} profile depend on?
questions.answer: It depends on the model data passed to the template resolver, including any placeholders future generation chooses to provide.

questions.question: Does this FAQ publish anything for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. The FAQ pool is passive data and does not publish, update, or render model pages by itself.

questions.question: Why use deterministic copy for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: Deterministic selection makes future model pages easier to reproduce, test, and review without random output changes.

questions.question: How is {{model_name}} verification handled?
questions.answer: Verification language should be based on confirmed destination signals and the model data available during future generation.

questions.question: Are verified links changed for {{model_name}} in this PR?
questions.answer: No. This PR does not change verified-link logic, destinations, routing, or any existing frontend output.

questions.question: What happens if {{model_name}} has no verified destination?
questions.answer: Future output should handle missing data conservatively and avoid implying verification that the source data does not support.

questions.question: Can the pool verify {{model_name}} by itself?
questions.answer: No. The pool only selects text templates and resolves placeholders; it does not perform research or validation.

questions.question: Does the pool update Rank Math for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. The pool does not write Rank Math fields, keywords, chips, schema, or metadata.

questions.question: Does template selection affect indexing for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. Template selection is isolated and does not touch indexing, noindex, canonical, or sitemap behavior.

questions.question: Why leave unknown placeholders visible for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: Leaving unknown placeholders visible makes missing data obvious during future development instead of silently hiding issues.

questions.question: Can empty verification data break {{model_name}} output?
questions.answer: The pool is designed to avoid fatal errors and modulo-by-zero cases when sections or FAQ buckets are empty.

questions.question: Does this class call external APIs for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. It loads local JSON data only and performs deterministic selection in memory.

questions.question: Is {{model_name}} verification rendered now?
questions.answer: No. Rendering will be handled in a future PR that wires the pool into selected model generation paths.

questions.question: Where can readers find {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: Future generation should answer from verified model destinations and platform data, not from assumptions in the template pool.

questions.question: Does this PR change affiliate links for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. Affiliate routing and link-building behavior are not touched by the template-pool foundation.

questions.question: Does the pool choose platforms for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. It can phrase platform context, but platform choice must come from the existing model data and generation logic.

questions.question: Can platform copy vary for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: Yes. Once wired, deterministic post-ID selection can rotate platform wording without random behavior.

questions.question: Does this PR change video generation for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. Video generation paths and templates are outside the scope of this foundation PR.

questions.question: Does this PR touch category generation for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. Category generation is not modified or called by the template pool.

questions.question: Can {{model_name}} have multiple platform notes?
questions.answer: Future wiring may pass platform data into placeholders, but this class only resolves what it receives.

questions.question: Does platform wording for {{model_name}} render immediately?
questions.answer: No. The class is registered for future use but is not connected to live model-page rendering.

questions.question: How are platform placeholders for {{model_name}} handled?
questions.answer: Known placeholders are replaced from the provided model data, while unknown placeholders remain visible.

questions.question: Can missing platform data fatal for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: The pool avoids fatals for missing keys and skips empty FAQ buckets safely.

questions.question: How current is {{model_name}} information?
questions.answer: Freshness depends on the data supplied by the existing plugin systems when future generation uses these templates.

questions.question: Does this PR refresh {{model_name}} pages?
questions.answer: No. It does not regenerate, publish, or update any model pages.

questions.question: Can {{model_name}} platform status change?
questions.answer: Yes. Model destinations and activity signals can change, so future output should remain evidence-aware.

questions.question: Does the pool store freshness data for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. It stores reusable copy patterns and FAQ entries, not live status or audit records.

questions.question: Can stale placeholders hide {{model_name}} data issues?
questions.answer: Unknown placeholders remain visible so missing mappings can be spotted during development and review.

questions.question: Does this PR schedule refreshes for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. It does not add cron jobs, queues, or background publication behavior.

questions.question: Will {{model_name}} output change after activation?
questions.answer: No. Loading the class and data files alone does not alter existing model-page output.

questions.question: How should future {{model_name}} freshness text be selected?
questions.answer: Future wiring can select templates deterministically from the post ID and supplied model data.

questions.question: Does this pool read remote freshness signals for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: No. It reads local JSON files only and performs no network requests.

questions.question: Can freshness FAQ buckets be empty for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: If a bucket has no questions, the pool skips it instead of selecting from an empty array.

questions.question: How will {{model_name}} templates be used later?
questions.answer: A future PR can call the pool from model generation for selected test models while preserving existing safeguards.

questions.question: How many FAQs are returned for {{model_name}} by default?
questions.answer: The pool returns one FAQ per bucket by default, up to the requested per-page limit.

questions.question: How are FAQ variants chosen for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: FAQ questions are selected deterministically using the post ID and bucket position.

questions.question: How are section variants chosen for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: Section variants are selected deterministically using the post ID and section key.

questions.question: Can developers request all {{model_name}} sections?
questions.answer: Yes. The public API includes a method to return all configured sections with placeholders resolved.

questions.question: Can developers inspect section keys for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: Yes. The pool exposes section keys so future generation can choose sections explicitly.

questions.question: Can developers inspect FAQ bucket keys for {{model_name}}?
questions.answer: Yes. The pool exposes FAQ bucket keys for predictable future wiring and tests.

questions.question: Does the pool escape {{model_name}} output?
questions.answer: No. Escaping is intentionally left to future render-time code where the output context is known.

questions.question: Does the pool sanitize {{model_name}} data?
questions.answer: No. This class is limited to data selection and placeholder resolution.

questions.question: Can the pool be tested for {{model_name}} without frontend output?
questions.answer: Yes. It can be validated from PHP syntax checks and local JSON parsing commands.
